stories.headline: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

stories.headline: Ukraine hits oil and military facilities near Russia’s St Petersburg
stories.summary: Kyiv's drones disrupt St Petersburg internet and flights as Russian strikes halt a gas facility in central Ukraine.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes oil infrastructure sites near Saint Petersburg, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: It comes after Moscow launched a massive drone and missile barrage at Kyiv earlier this week, killing at least 30 people and hitting more than 20 sites across the city.

stories.headline: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
stories.summary: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSFFatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but said the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far.The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can liter

stories.headline: At least 7 Palestinians killed by Israeli forces over past 48 hours in Gaza
stories.summary: A further nine bodies were recovered from under the rubble, according to Gaza's Ministry of Health.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy Denies Putin Claim of Securing Donetsk Stronghold
stories.summary: Ukraine pushed back on a claim by Russian President Vladimir Putin that Kremlin forces have taken control of Kostiantynivka, a strategic logistics center in the Donetsk region, saying the heavily bombarded city remains under Ukrainian command.

stories.headline: Thousands gather as Khamenei funeral ceremonies open in Tehran
stories.summary: Thousands of mourners attended the public grand procession of Iran’s late Supreme Leader Khamenei in Tehran.

stories.headline: Keir Starmer calls decision to resign as British PM 'intensely personal'
stories.summary: Starmer also said the next prime minister would not be able to spend any less time focusing on diplomacy and international affairs, citing the impact of the wars in Ukraine and Iran on the cost of living.

stories.headline: Pope makes historic visit to Lampedusa, warns Europe: 'Deaths at sea are failures to act'
stories.summary: Pope Francis has visited the island that symbolises the migrant crisis. Greeted by thousands, he stressed Europe’s duty to act and urged the faithful not to ignore the suffering of those forced to leave their homeland.

stories.headline: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

stories.headline: Dior dress, Adam Sandler and a man of honour: What we know about Taylor and Travis's wedding
stories.summary: The couple tied the knot in New York on Friday, after months of speculation about when, where and how it would happen.

stories.headline: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
stories.summary: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

stories.headline: BBC in Tehran as mourners gather for former supreme leader's funeral
stories.summary: Iranian authorities say they expect millions of people to take part in funeral commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.headline: Trump uses Mount Rushmore speech to warn of ‘communist menace’ in US
stories.summary: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections

stories.headline: NY Harbor Celebration Echoes Revolutionary Past
stories.summary: For the 4th of July, the Hudson River will have both the Naval Review and 'Sail4th 250' to celebrate the day. Retired Naval Vice Admiral Kevin Donegan and Rutgers University-Newark Professor of American Studies Robert Snyder join Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the historical significance of the Hudson River in American independence. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Air Force Detains Officer Who Called for Trump’s Impeachment at Capitol
stories.summary: Maj. Jason Watson, who was in uniform, was arrested during a protest that followed a news conference on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Sydney records hottest June since 1859 as expert warns new high a ‘signature’ of global warming
stories.summary: Bureau of Meteorology says city’s mean temperature reached 16.1C, surpassing the previous record of 15.7C set in 1991Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastSydney has officially experienced its hottest June since records began, after a winter month of spring-like warmth.While early weather data suggested the month was merely among the warmest, the Bureau of Meteorology confirmed on Friday that June 2026 broke the all-time mean

stories.headline: German riot police clash with protesters hoping to block far-right AfD conference
stories.summary: Thousands of police deployed to Erfurt in central Germany as party holds conference on key Nazi dateRiot police have clashed with opponents of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party on the streets of Erfurt in Germany, where thousands met to block roads and prevent AfD delegates from attending the party’s biennial national conference to elect its leadership.Police reported 20,000 protesters were demonstrating in the eastern city, w

stories.headline: Pope praises US history of welcoming migrants in implicit rebuke to Trump
stories.summary: Pope Leo urges Americans to live up to ideals of Declaration of Independence in first key address to USPope Leo has used his first key address to his home country to praise the US history of ⁠welcoming migrants, urging Americans to live up to the ideals put forward in the ⁠Declaration of Independence.In his latest implicit rebuke to Donald Trump, the first US leader of the Roman Catholic church said the word “America” had become ‌a “byword for fr

stories.headline: Police clash with protesters outside AfD meeting
stories.summary: German police scuffled with protesters as the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) held its national convention

stories.headline: Thousands protest in Germany as far-right AfD party meets
stories.summary: Protesters from unions, civil society and left-wing parties aim to disrupt AfD annual conference in Erfurt.

stories.headline: Trump warns of communist threat as Washington parade cancelled due to heatwave
stories.summary: "Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty," Trump said during a speech on Friday. "It is the greatest threat to our country".

stories.headline: 20,000 protest against AfD party conference in Germany
stories.summary: Thüringen police described the protest as "legitimate" but said an AfD constituency office and police officers had been attacked with paint bombs and fireworks.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Yankee pyramids: how to build a presidential library
stories.summary: Every modern US president gets a physical totem of his tenure. What can they teach us about America’s struggle to remember itself?

stories.headline: Brazil’s Durigan Says Credit Lines Won’t Affect Monetary Policy
stories.summary: Credit measures introduced by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s government do not undermine monetary policy, Finance Minister Dario Durigan said in an interview with local news website G1 published on Saturday.

stories.headline: Moore: American Dream Still Worth Fighting For
stories.summary: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore said patriotism should unite rather than divide Americans, arguing that service and sacrifice—not politics—define love of country and remain essential to fulfilling the nation's founding promise. While speaking with Bloomberg This Weekend hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini Moore also urged leaders to focus on delivering tangible results on affordability, education and economic opportunity, saying voters are

stories.headline: Coney Island Boardwalk Set for $1 Billion Upgrade
stories.summary: The executive director of the Alliance for Coney Island Daniel Murphy joins Lisa Mateo of Bloomberg This Weekend to explain how Coney Island is entering a new phase of growth with a permanent business improvement district and a planned $1 billion investment to rebuild the boardwalk and support local businesses. He says the upgrades are intended to strengthen Coney Island as a year-round community while preserving its identity as "the people's pla

stories.headline: Putin Signs Tax Amendments Aiming to Boost Domestic Fuel Supply
stories.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a law to stimulate gasoline supplies to the domestic market.

stories.headline: Polls Say American Pride Depends on How You Ask
stories.summary: Between June 8th and 11th, NPR/PBS/Marist conducted a poll of 1,340 adults and found thatAmericans remain broadly proud of their country, but views on patriotism, the nation's future and trust in government are increasingly divided along partisan lines, even as overall levels of national pride have changed little over time. Director at the Eagleton Center for Public Interest at Rutgers Ashley Connan and Puck News writer Abby Livingston join Bloom

stories.headline: David Rubenstein Talks Legacy with "Thomas Jefferson"
stories.summary: For the 4th of July, Bloomberg's David Rubenstein sat down with Bill Barker, a historical actor-interpreter of Thomas Jefferson. As part of the Thomas Jefferson Foundation at Monticello, Barker discusses with Rubenstein, in character as Thomas Jefferson, the founding fathers public service, legacy, and symbolism. Watch the interview on Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Insider Trading Cases End as DOJ Dud With Few Prison Sentences
stories.summary: Sitting at home in the south of France, beyond the reach of US law enforcement for the better part of a decade, former Moelis and Co. banker Benjamin Taylor made what seemed like a brave choice. He returned to the US to face an insider trading charge that carried as much as five years behind bars.

stories.headline: Celebrating '250' from the USS Intrepid
stories.summary: To celebrate America's 250th anniversary Bloomberg This Weekend is broadcasting Live from across New York City. David Gura and Christina Ruffini are on the Hudson River at the Intrepid Museum for the Navy Review and the tall ships parade, 'Sail4th 250', while Lisa Mateo is at Nathan's Famous Hot Dogs in Coney Island, home of the annual 4th of July hot dog eating contest. The team are joined by Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason w

stories.headline: Credit Agricole Increases BPM Stake, Boosting Position in Italy
stories.summary: The Credit Agricole SA Campus in Paris, France, on Monday, Feb. 2, 2026. The euro-area economy grew more than expected at the end of last year, demonstrating resilience to the trade turmoil unleashed by Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Modi Says India Will Keep Expanding Oil Refining Capacity
stories.summary: India will continue to build new crude oil refineries in order to ensure supply chain security even as Western nations shut processing units, Prime Minister Narendra Modi said.

stories.headline: Nearly a Million Investors Lost a Total of $3.8 Billion on Trump Crypto Coin
stories.summary: A report from a cryptocurrency analytics firm details how those who bought the Trump memecoin have fared, with most retail investors having lost money while sophisticated traders did better.

stories.headline: How Do You Celebrate America’s 250-Year Experiment? However You Want.
stories.summary: Away from the turmoil in Washington, Americans will mark July 4 in their own patriotic ways. Expect rodeos, line dancing, Tejano music and Led Zeppelin.

stories.headline: UK woman, 21, arrested in Thailand after allegedly stabbing boyfriend to death
stories.summary: Officers found body of 34-year-old man in luxury rental home in Pattaya area, local media saysA 21-year-old British woman has been arrested in Thailand after allegedly fatally stabbing her boyfriend, according to local media reports.The Bangkok Post reported that on Thursday morning local time, officers found the body of a 34-year-old man, who operated a cannabis farm, in a luxury rental home in the Pattaya area, a beachside region two hours from

Japan's clothing imports from China hit 31-year low in Southeast Asia shift
South Korea's tungsten mine revival seeks China-free supply
Japan's corporate reform genie will not go back in the bottle

At least 32 dead after overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in Pakistan
stories.summary: The bus was travelling from Quetta to Peshawar, when it crashed in the Dana Sar mountain range.

stories.headline: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. stories.headline: New pipeline in Canada to proceed after C$150bn pledged to ease BC and First Nations concerns
stories.summary: Port expansion and protections for whales part of BC and Alberta plan to expand country’s presence overseasThe governments of Canada and the province of Alberta will move forward on a major new oil pipeline after the pair announced a plan to ease concerns of British Columbia and First Nations on the Pacific coast.Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, shuttled between British Columbia and Alberta on Thursday to announce more than C$150bn in new in

stories.headline: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape
stories.summary: Thousands of volunteers are joined by overseas teams in the hope of finding more survivors in the rubble, reports Tom Phillips in Caraballeda.Photography and video by Manu QuinteroWhen twin earthquakes tore through Venezuela’s northern coast last week, Israel Rivas was at home hundreds of miles away in the industrial city of San Félix. As the scale of the catastrophe became clear, the 24-year-old knew he had to react. A mechanic and budding photo

stories.headline: China says man who flew plane into Beijing skyscraper had mental health problems
stories.summary: Official statement offers the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual and fatal incident in BeijingChinese authorities said the man who flew a small plane into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper last week was a 66-year-old who had mental health problems.The statement published on Thursday offered the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual incident that occurred in Beijing’s central business district on the evening of

stories.headline: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
stories.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

stories.headline: Dissident Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee dies aged 70
stories.summary: Lam Wing-kee, who defied China by selling material critical of Beijing, passed away after suffering from lung cancer.

stories.headline: Cold feet and cooling prices: Australia’s property market is transforming – and first home buyers aren’t biting
stories.summary: Exclusive: Data shows buyers are not rushing to get into the market, despite lower prices and less competitionGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastFirst home buyers are starting to step back and investor demand is slumping in all areas except new property, as Australia’s housing market enters a downturn.Research shows the market is transforming, nearly two months on from a third consecutive interest rate rise and sweeping ta

stories.headline: One Nation is campaigning directly to Christians. But will party policies rub against worshippers’ conscience?
stories.summary: Anti-abortion policies may have appeal, but with one in three Australian churchgoers born overseas, talk of a monoculture may put them offGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWhen One Nation recruit Barnaby Joyce addressed anti-abortion campaigners at a Sydney rally in early June, the former deputy prime minister told the audience he could see “about 1,500 people who can hand out how to vote cards”.Christian leaders spoke at

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats
stories.summary: Sarah Rainsford reports from the completely destroyed nine-storey block of flats in Ukraine's capital.

stories.headline: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
stories.summary: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and NigeriaFloods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan.There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ caseDoctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness.In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-ye

stories.headline: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?
stories.summary: Rights groups fear the new law gives Beijing legal authority to go after people beyond its borders.

stories.headline: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
stories.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Caraballeda, La Guaira, where locals are still desperately searching for family members in the rubble.

stories.headline: Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
stories.summary: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Dogs, drones and sound detectors: How rescuers search for quake survivors
stories.summary: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: Big oil’s secretive trading arms are having an extraordinary year
stories.summary: Meet the corporate gamblers who never waste a good energy crisis

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
stories.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.
